novel_features.name: Cross-platform presence matrix
novel_features.description: Given a single handle, see which of 11+ platforms the creator is on, with follower counts side-by-side.
novel_features.rationale: Requires fanning out to every profile endpoint and joining results — no single API call answers this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs a creator's full footprint before writing outreach or analysis, this returns it in one call instead of 11.

novel_features.name: Trend triangulation across platforms
novel_features.description: Snapshot a hashtag or keyword on TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, and Threads in one call to see which platform it's rising fastest on.
novel_features.rationale: Joins per-platform search endpoints with a normalized count delta from the local snapshot table. No single endpoint exposes cross-platform trend velocity.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Marketers asking 'is this trend cresting on TikTok' can see the answer plus the leading-indicator platform in one command.

novel_features.name: Transcript full-text search across 5 platforms
novel_features.description: FTS5-indexed search across all transcripts you've synced — TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, and Twitter videos.
novel_features.rationale: Batch-fetches and indexes transcripts locally; the API returns one transcript per call, so cross-creator/cross-platform search is only possible after sync.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents doing brand-mention or keyword-monitoring across video content can grep their entire synced corpus offline.

novel_features.name: Ad library unified search
novel_features.description: Search Facebook, Google, and LinkedIn ad libraries in one query — see every ad a brand is currently running.
novel_features.rationale: Three separate endpoints (`facebook/adLibrary/search/companies`, `google/adLibrary/advertisers/search`, `linkedin/ads/search`) joined and deduplicated by company.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Competitive intel without juggling three vendors and three APIs.

novel_features.name: Engagement spike detector
novel_features.description: Find videos that performed significantly above a creator's average — the ones that actually went viral.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the creator's full content history with view/like counts in the local store to compute the baseline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When asked 'which videos took off,' the agent can answer with statistical confidence instead of guessing.

novel_features.name: Multi-creator comparison
novel_features.description: Compare two or more creators side-by-side on follower count, engagement rate, posting cadence, and content volume.
novel_features.rationale: Needs synced profile + content data; a single API call doesn't return comparative stats.
novel_features.example: scrape-creators-pp-cli creator compare mrbeast pewdiepie --platform youtube --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Influencer-shortlist work that used to take a spreadsheet now takes one command.

novel_features.name: Follower growth tracker
novel_features.description: Snapshot a creator's follower count daily across every platform; chart their growth trajectory over time.
novel_features.rationale: Daily snapshots in SQLite reveal trends no single API call provides.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Trend lines for momentum decisions (sponsorship, partnership timing).

novel_features.name: Content cadence analysis
novel_features.description: See when a creator posts — by day of week and hour — across every platform you've synced for them.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregated post timestamps from the local store; the API returns posts but not posting-pattern statistics.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Benchmark a competitor's publishing strategy or recommend the right slot for a creator partner.

novel_features.name: Engagement rate ranker
novel_features.description: Rank a creator's synced content by engagement rate (not raw likes) so you see their true best performers.
novel_features.rationale: Computes (likes+comments+shares)/views per item from local data; engagement rate isn't a field the API returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces over-performers that raw view counts hide.

novel_features.name: Trend delta tracker
novel_features.description: Track whether a hashtag is growing or shrinking by comparing video counts across snapshot intervals.
novel_features.rationale: Snapshot table in SQLite + scheduled snapshots; a single API call returns a count, not a trend.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Distinguish a stable hashtag from a rising or dying one in seconds.

novel_features.name: Brand ad campaign monitor
novel_features.description: Snapshot a brand's ads on Facebook, Google, and LinkedIn into SQLite; on rerun, diff new ads vs ones that disappeared.
novel_features.rationale: Requires persistent storage of past ad fingerprints; the API returns a current list but no diff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Cron-friendly competitive monitoring without glue code.

novel_features.name: Link-in-bio universal resolver
novel_features.description: Paste any linktree.ee, komi.io, pillar.io, linkbio, or linkme URL and get the unified destination list.
novel_features.rationale: Auto-detects the service from the URL and dispatches to the right endpoint; five endpoints unified behind one command.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lead-research tasks that need a creator's full link footprint stop caring which bio service they used.

novel_features.name: Credit burn rate monitor
novel_features.description: See how fast you're spending API credits and how many days remain at current pace, fused with the API's own usage history.
novel_features.rationale: Local `usage_log` table + the API's `get-api-usage` and `get-most-used-routes` endpoints; neither alone is sufficient for projection.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catches runaway sync jobs before they exhaust the plan.

narrative.headline: Every Scrape Creators endpoint, plus offline search, cross-platform compounding, and a local store no other Scrape Creators tool ships with.
narrative.value_prop: Wrap all 114 Scrape Creators endpoints across 23 platforms — TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, Reddit, Threads, Bluesky, Pinterest, and more — and add a SQLite-backed store with full-text transcript search, cross-platform presence checks, ad-library unified search, and credit-burn projection. Read-only across the board, agent-native by default, every command a typed MCP tool.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set SCRAPE_CREATORS_API_KEY (get one at https://scrapecreators.com). One header, no OAuth handshake.

quickstart.comment: Verify auth and see remaining credits.
Cross-platform presence — see every platform a creator is on.
Pull a single profile (any of 23 platforms supported).
Persist tiktok resources to local SQLite (use --resources for any platform).
FTS5 across every synced transcript, offline.
Unified Facebook + Google + LinkedIn ad library search.

troubleshoots.fix: Set SCRAPE_CREATORS_API_KEY in your environment or run `scrape-creators-pp-cli auth set-token <key>`.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty result for a profile that should exist
troubleshoots.fix: Some platforms require a username, others a handle prefixed with @, and a few need the numeric ID. Check the command's `--help` for the exact arg shape.

troubleshoots.symptom: Credits running out faster than expected
troubleshoots.fix: Run `scrape-creators-pp-cli account budget --json` — it shows daily spend and projects days remaining.
Transcripts must be synced first — run `scrape-creators-pp-cli sync --resources tiktok` (or any platform with transcripts) and try again.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs public social-media data across multiple platforms in one invocation, when you need offline search over previously fetched content, or when you want cross-platform compound queries (presence, ads, trends) that no single API call answers. It's read-only — pair it with a posting tool if you need write capability.

recipes.title: Map a creator's full footprint
recipes.explanation: Probes every platform's profile endpoint and returns a presence matrix with follower counts. Useful before writing a brief or outreach email.

recipes.title: Find a creator's viral hits
recipes.command: scrape-creators-pp-cli sync --resources youtube && scrape-creators-pp-cli content spikes mrbeast --threshold 2.0 --platform youtube --json
recipes.explanation: Sync videos to local SQLite, then return videos whose engagement is more than 2× the creator's average.

recipes.title: Track a brand's ad campaigns across networks
recipes.explanation: Snapshots Facebook + Google + LinkedIn ads into SQLite; rerun on a cron and diff new vs disappeared.

recipes.title: Triangulate where a trend is rising
recipes.explanation: Probes TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, and Threads for a topic and returns per-platform velocity. Reads the leading platform when run repeatedly.

recipes.title: Grep your transcript corpus
recipes.explanation: FTS5 over every transcript you've synced, returning the creator/platform/URL/match — useful for brand-safety audits.
